title: JWT secret keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects hardcoded string literals passed to Encoding.GetBytes() when used with JWT security classes
logic.triggering_conditions: (Encoding.GetBytes with string literal) AND (SymmetricSecurityKey OR SigningCredentials OR JwtSecurityToken context)
logic.reasoning: JWT secret keys must never be hardcoded in source code as they pose a critical security risk. This rule detects when JWT-related security classes receive hardcoded string literals via Encoding.GetBytes(). The check searches for patterns where SymmetricSecurityKey, SigningCredentials, or JwtSecurityToken are constructed with hardcoded strings passed to GetBytes(). Exclusions prevent false positives from configuration retrieval, logging, tests, and comments. Self-critique identified 3 potential false positives: (1) test fixtures with dummy keys - excluded via [Test]/[Fact], (2) logging secret metadata (not values) - excluded via Console.*/Logger.*, (3) documentation examples in comments - excluded via ^//, ^/*, ^///. Edge cases: (1) secrets in base64 - still triggers as GetBytes("literal") detected, (2) async configuration loading - compliant as Configuration[] pattern excluded.

should_trigger:
var key = Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes("MySecretKey123456");
var signingCredentials = new SigningCredentials(new SymmetricSecurityKey(key), SecurityAlgorithms.HmacSha256);
var token = new JwtSecurityToken(signingCredentials: new SigningCredentials(new SymmetricSecurityKey(Encoding.ASCII.GetBytes("secret")), SecurityAlgorithms.HmacSha256));
should_not_trigger:
var key = Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(Configuration["JwtSettings:SecretKey"]);
var signingCredentials = new SigningCredentials(new SymmetricSecurityKey(key), SecurityAlgorithms.HmacSha256);
